claim_ceiling: Functional Subject response-attribution scorecard local workflow pass
what_changed: Functional Subject reports now include response_attribution_summary with origin counts, clean first-pass rate, repair case ids, repair type counts, terminal/tool/provider case ids, and per-case origins.
GPT-5.5 judge packets include the same scorecard and a stricter judging instruction to separate first-pass LLM behavior, runtime guard behavior, and end-to-end operator behavior.
Experiment-control summary and Markdown reports surface the scorecard for evidence closeout.

acceptance_result.gate: GPT-5.5 judge packet includes the same scorecard and first-pass versus repair instruction.
Markdown report surfaces the scorecard compactly.
Existing Functional Subject trial and experiment-control tests do not regress.

verification.summary: provider-unavailable local report smoke; scorecard present in report, judge packet, and Markdown

residuals: This task does not reduce runtime repair dependence; it makes that dependence measurable.
EGO-FS-010 still requires real-provider/human evidence for closeout.
Future tasks should use the scorecard to decide whether to improve first-pass prompting, runtime guard behavior, or eval claims.
